steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
# Repair a Regressed CMake Build

I’ve got a small C++ project in `/app` that used to build fine, but after splitting it into `core`, `engine`, `plugin`, and `app`, the CMake setup got messy. The source files themselves are fine; the breakage is in the CMake wiring.

Please fix the build configuration using only `CMakeLists.txt` changes. Don’t edit any `.cpp`, `.h`, or `.in` files.

What I need working again is pretty straightforward from the outside:
- clean out-of-source builds for both `Release` and `Debug`
- clean builds should also work with the `Ninja` generator for both `Release` and `Debug`
- both executables (`myapp` and `selfcheck`) must build and run
- `myapp` should print:

```
text: HELLO
hash: 0x05e918d2
valid: yes
profile: <build type>
```

- `selfcheck` should print:

```
selfcheck: ok (<build type>)
```

The build-profile header should be generated per build type (so Release and Debug runs show the right profile). Also make sure dependency wiring is clean: `core` headers should be exported correctly, and `engine` should declare its dependency on `core` and thread support by using CMake’s Threads package target.

Please keep language standard settings target-scoped (per target compile features) rather than a single global project-wide C++ standard. Concretely:

- Do **not** set `CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD` (or similar) at the top level; remove any project-wide C++ standard if present.
- The **`core`** and **`plugin`** libraries are small and only need **C++17**: add `target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)` and `target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)`.
- The **`engine`** static library and both executables use C++20 features in the existing sources: add `target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)`, `target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)`, and `target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)` so those translation units build as C++20 (this should show up in `compile_commands.json` for `engine/engine.cpp` and `app/main.cpp` when you export compile commands).

Export `core`’s public headers with `target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ...)`, and have `engine` link to the `core` target (so include paths propagate). You may split CMake arguments across lines; keep **`core`** and **`PUBLIC`** as the first two arguments to `target_include_directories` for the `core` target.

For threads, in **`engine/CMakeLists.txt`** call `find_package(Threads REQUIRED)` and link **`Threads::Threads`** to `engine` (that satisfies “CMake’s Threads package target”).

One more thing: keep `plugin` as an object-library-style component in the final setup.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@jour-VMware-Virtual-Platform:/app#

Analysis: We are in /app but no commands have been run yet, so we haven't inspected the current (broken) CMake layout. To fix the build using only CMakeLists.txt changes, we first need to inspect the repository structure and all CMakeLists.txt files to see how targets (core/engine/plugin/app) are currently wired, where the generated profile header is configured, and what executables/targets exist.
Plan: 1) Inspect the directory tree and locate all CMakeLists.txt files. 2) Open the top-level and subdirectory CMakeLists.txt files to understand current targets, include dirs, link deps, and any global C++ standard settings that must be removed. 3) After inspection, we will edit only CMakeLists.txt files to: add proper add_subdirectory structure; ensure core exports headers via target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ...); make engine link to core and Threads::Threads (with find_package(Threads REQUIRED) in engine/CMakeLists.txt); keep plugin as an object library; generate build-profile header per build type; set target-scoped compile features (core/plugin cxx_std_17; engine/myapp/selfcheck cxx_std_20). Then we will validate with out-of-source Debug/Release builds (Unix Makefiles and Ninja) and run myapp/selfcheck to confirm output.

steps.message:
Analysis: Top-level CMakeLists.txt incorrectly sets a global C++ standard (CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17), and subprojects are minimally wired. core exports includes as PRIVATE (should be PUBLIC). engine doesn’t link to core or Threads::Threads, and only has PRIVATE includes. plugin is correctly an OBJECT library but only has PRIVATE includes and lacks required compile features. app builds only myapp; selfcheck executable is missing. The per-build-type generated header (app/build_profile.h.in) is present but not configured/used by CMake right now. Also, app links myapp only to engine; likely needs plugin object files and proper include dirs to reach generated header and other headers.
Plan: 1) Inspect sources briefly (without editing) to confirm how build_profile.h is included and how targets depend on each other (core/engine/plugin). 2) Fix CMake wiring:
- Remove global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD settings from top-level.
- Reorder add_subdirectory so libraries are defined before app.
- core: export headers with target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ...) and add target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17).
- engine: add find_package(Threads REQUIRED); link to core and Threads::Threads; export its include dir as PUBLIC (so apps can include engine headers); add target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20).
- plugin: keep as OBJECT, add target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17), and make its include dir PUBLIC so consumers can include plugin.h.
- app: configure_file(build_profile.h.in -> build_profile.h) per build dir; ensure app target can include the binary dir; add executables myapp and selfcheck; link myapp to engine and add plugin object files via $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>; link selfcheck to engine too; apply target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20) and same for selfcheck.
3) Validate with out-of-source builds for Debug/Release with Ninja and default generator; run both executables and confirm output includes correct profile per build type.

Analysis: Sources confirm: engine uses C++20 features (std::span), and uses <thread> so engine must link Threads::Threads. app includes build_profile.h which must be generated from build_profile.h.in using configure_file per build directory, and app targets need include access to the generated file (binary dir). myapp and selfcheck both include engine.h and plugin.h, so their include paths must reach engine and plugin headers (ideally via PUBLIC include dirs from those targets). plugin provides free functions (to_hex, banner) and should remain an OBJECT library whose object files are added to executables. core’s headers must be PUBLIC so engine can include core.h transitively via linking to core.
Plan: Edit only CMakeLists.txt files:
1) Top-level: remove global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD settings; reorder add_subdirectory so core/engine/plugin come before app.
2) core: target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}); add target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17).
3) engine: add find_package(Threads REQUIRED); make engine include dir PUBLIC; link engine PUBLIC core and Threads::Threads; add target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20).
4) plugin: keep OBJECT; set include dir PUBLIC; add target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17).
5) app: configure_file(build_profile.h.in -> build_profile.h) in the app binary dir; add selfcheck executable; make both executables include ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}; link to engine; add plugin object files via target_sources(... $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>); add target_compile_features(myapp/selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20).
Then run fresh out-of-source builds for Debug/Release with Ninja and default generator and execute myapp/selfcheck to confirm expected output and correct profile string.

arguments.keystrokes:
apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: CMakeLists.txt
@@
 cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
 project(CryptoEngine CXX)
 
-set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
-set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
-
-add_subdirectory(app)
 add_subdirectory(core)
 add_subdirectory(engine)
 add_subdirectory(plugin)
+add_subdirectory(app)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: core/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(core STATIC core.cpp)
-target_include_directories(core PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: engine/CMakeLists.txt
@@
+find_package(Threads REQUIRED)
+
 add_library(engine STATIC engine.cpp)
-target_include_directories(engine PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_include_directories(engine PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_link_libraries(engine PUBLIC core Threads::Threads)
+target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: plugin/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(plugin OBJECT plugin.cpp)
-target_include_directories(plugin PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_include_directories(plugin PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: app/CMakeLists.txt
@@
-add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
-target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
+configure_file(
+    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/build_profile.h.in
+    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/build_profile.h
+    @ONLY
+)
+
+add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
+target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
+target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
+target_sources(myapp PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
+target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
+
+add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
+target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
+target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine)
+target_sources(selfcheck PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
+target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
*** End Patch
PATCH

steps.message:
Analysis: The patching failed because `apply_patch` is not available in this environment, so none of the intended CMake changes were applied. Builds therefore still use the old wiring: engine can’t find core.h (core include dir not propagated / not linked), app can’t find engine.h, and C++ standard is still gnu++17. We need to edit the CMakeLists.txt files using available tools (e.g., heredoc with cat > file, or sed).
Plan: Rewrite each CMakeLists.txt completely using cat heredocs (safe given they are short):
1) Top-level: remove global C++ standard settings; add subdirectories in dependency order core/engine/plugin/app.
2) core: make include dir PUBLIC and add C++17 compile features.
3) engine: find Threads, make include dir PUBLIC, link PUBLIC to core and Threads::Threads, and require C++20.
4) plugin: keep OBJECT, include dir PUBLIC, require C++17.
5) app: configure build_profile.h from .in; define myapp and selfcheck; include binary dir for generated header; link to engine; add plugin object files to both executables; set C++20 features on both executables.
Then re-configure and rebuild out-of-source for Debug/Release with Ninja and default generator and run both executables to confirm outputs and profile.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(CryptoEngine CXX)

add_subdirectory(core)
add_subdirectory(engine)
add_subdirectory(plugin)
add_subdirectory(app)
EOF

cat > /app/core/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(core STATIC core.cpp)

target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
EOF

cat > /app/engine/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
find_package(Threads REQUIRED)

add_library(engine STATIC engine.cpp)

target_include_directories(engine PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
target_link_libraries(engine PUBLIC core Threads::Threads)
target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)
EOF

cat > /app/plugin/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(plugin OBJECT plugin.cpp)

target_include_directories(plugin PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
EOF

cat > /app/app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
configure_file(
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/build_profile.h.in
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/build_profile.h
    @ONLY
)

add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
target_sources(myapp PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)

add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine)
target_sources(selfcheck PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
EOF

steps.message:
Analysis: Build now progresses through core/engine/plugin, but app compilation fails because plugin.h is not found. Although plugin exports its include dir PUBLIC, the executables don’t link to the plugin target, so they don’t inherit plugin’s include directories. Adding $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin> only adds object files, not usage requirements. We must either link the plugin target (even if it’s OBJECT) to propagate includes, or explicitly add /app/plugin as an include directory for the executables. Best is target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE plugin) and same for selfcheck; CMake allows linking to OBJECT libraries for usage requirements.
Plan: Update app/CMakeLists.txt to link myapp and selfcheck against the plugin object library target so plugin’s PUBLIC include directory propagates. Keep also target_sources with $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin> to actually include the object files in the final link. Then rebuild the existing out-of-source directories and run the executables for Debug/Release and Ninja Debug/Release.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
configure_file(
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/build_profile.h.in
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/build_profile.h
    @ONLY
)

add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine plugin)
target_sources(myapp PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)

add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine plugin)
target_sources(selfcheck PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
EOF

steps.message:
Analysis: All required builds now succeed out-of-source for Debug/Release with both default generator and Ninja. Both executables run and print the expected output including correct per-build-type profile. CMake wiring is fixed: core exports headers publicly, engine links to core and Threads::Threads, plugin remains an OBJECT library and is consumed by executables with propagated includes, and language standards are target-scoped via target_compile_features with no global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD.
Plan: Task is complete. No further commands are required.

steps.message:
Analysis: Yes. The project now builds cleanly out-of-source in both Debug and Release using both the default generator and Ninja. Both executables (myapp and selfcheck) build and run successfully, and their output matches the required text including the correct per-build-type profile string generated from app/build_profile.h.in. CMake requirements are satisfied: no global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD is set; core/plugin use cxx_std_17; engine and both executables use cxx_std_20; core exports headers PUBLIC; engine links PUBLIC to core and Threads::Threads with find_package(Threads REQUIRED) in engine/CMakeLists.txt; plugin remains an OBJECT library and is consumed properly.
Plan: No further changes; mark complete.
